items.title: Team Onboarding Paved Road: operating discovery
items.name: Team Onboarding Paved Road: operating discovery
items.summary: Discovery work is testing whether team onboarding paved road can move the linked customer and business KPIs while addressing making the paved road faster and safer while keeping support and infrastructure cost flat.
items.opportunity: Clarify whether this bet is strong enough to scale in the next planning cycle, or whether the organization should narrow scope and protect focus.

linkedInitiatives.description: Team Onboarding Paved Road exists to Make the default team setup path fast enough that product teams stop treating onboarding as a local project. The quarter concentrates on paved-road adoption, default guardrails, AI-era runtime readiness, and cost visibility so the work reads like a realistic OKR-driven investment set for a company with roughly 250 product and technology people.

assignments.how: Leads the discovery framing, evidence synthesis, and recommendation on how far this initiative should scale.
Adds workflow, commercial, and implementation evidence needed to close the main delivery risks.
Adds workflow, commercial, and implementation evidence needed to close the main delivery risks.

riskFocus.question: Will customers buy the product and/or choose to use it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Customers will engage more consistently if team onboarding paved road measurably improves median team onboarding time.
evidenceToCollect: Whether target users describe median team onboarding time as a top-priority pain point.
Whether the proposed workflow changes are strong enough to change behavior, not just preference.

riskFocus.question: Will the solution meet the needs of the business?
riskFocus.hypothesis: The investment is worth scaling when it improves platform adoption across product teams without creating disproportionate operating cost or support load.
evidenceToCollect: Which economic or operating constraints cap the upside.
What minimum KPI movement would justify continued investment.

riskFocus.question: Can customers figure out how to use it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Users can complete the redesigned workflow with less guidance and less back-and-forth than the current path.
evidenceToCollect: Where users hesitate in the new flow.
Which messages, states, or transitions remain unclear.

riskFocus.question: Can we build it within given resources and constraints?
riskFocus.hypothesis: The teams can deliver the smallest useful slice inside current architecture and staffing constraints without creating a long tail of one-off work.
evidenceToCollect: What can be standardized versus localized or custom.

plannedActivities: Compare adoption, time-to-value, and support demand across teams on and off the paved road.
Replay common exception journeys around identity, policy, and runtime provisioning.
Test whether more explicit cost and reliability signals change platform usage decisions.
items.decisionToMake: Decide which platform capabilities should become mandatory defaults next quarter and which still need product-quality improvement before wider rollout.

items.title: Runtime Delivery Self-Service: operating discovery
items.name: Runtime Delivery Self-Service: operating discovery
items.summary: Discovery work is testing whether runtime delivery self-service can move the linked customer and business KPIs while addressing making the paved road faster and safer while keeping support and infrastructure cost flat.
items.opportunity: Clarify whether this bet is strong enough to scale in the next planning cycle, or whether the organization should narrow scope and protect focus.

linkedInitiatives.description: Runtime Delivery Self-Service exists to Shorten the path from repo to production by making the standard runtime and delivery path more complete, visible, and self-service. The quarter concentrates on paved-road adoption, default guardrails, AI-era runtime readiness, and cost visibility so the work reads like a realistic OKR-driven investment set for a company with roughly 250 product and technology people.

assignments.how: Leads the discovery framing, evidence synthesis, and recommendation on how far this initiative should scale.
Adds workflow, commercial, and implementation evidence needed to close the main delivery risks.

riskFocus.question: Will customers buy the product and/or choose to use it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Customers will engage more consistently if runtime delivery self-service measurably improves time from repo creation to first production deployment.
evidenceToCollect: Whether target users describe time from repo creation to first production deployment as a top-priority pain point.
Whether the proposed workflow changes are strong enough to change behavior, not just preference.

riskFocus.question: Will the solution meet the needs of the business?
riskFocus.hypothesis: The investment is worth scaling when it improves lead time for change without creating disproportionate operating cost or support load.
evidenceToCollect: Which economic or operating constraints cap the upside.
What minimum KPI movement would justify continued investment.

riskFocus.hypothesis: Users can complete the redesigned workflow with less guidance and less back-and-forth than the current path.

Which messages, states, or transitions remain unclear.

riskFocus.hypothesis: The teams can deliver the smallest useful slice inside current architecture and staffing constraints without creating a long tail of one-off work.
evidenceToCollect: What can be standardized versus localized or custom.

plannedActivities: Compare adoption, time-to-value, and support demand across teams on and off the paved road.
Replay common exception journeys around identity, policy, and runtime provisioning.
Test whether more explicit cost and reliability signals change platform usage decisions.
